2604.25978.title: Mini-Batch Class Composition Bias in Link Prediction
2604.25978.paper_summary: Prior work on node classification has shown that Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) can learn representations that transfer across graphs, when underlying graph properties are shared. For a fixed graph, one would then expect GNNs trained for link prediction to learn a representation consistent with that learnt for node classification. We show this intuition does not hold in the general case. Instead, we find popular link prediction models can learn a trivial mini-batch dependent heuristic, enabled by batch-normalisation layers, to solve the edge classification task. When correcting for this, we observe increased alignment of the network representation with node-class relevant features, suggesting the network has learnt a graph representation that better aligns with the underlying graph's properties. Our findings suggest that standard link prediction training may be leading us to overestimate link predictors' ability to learn a generalised representation of a graph that is consistent across tasks.
2604.25978.paper_summary_zh: 先前關於節點分類的研究顯示，圖神經網絡（GNNs）可以學習在共享底層圖屬性的情況下跨圖轉移的表示。對於固定的圖，人們會期望為鏈接預測訓練的GNN能學習到與節點分類所學習的表示一致的表示。我們展示了這一直覺在一般情況下並不成立。相反，我們發現流行的鏈接預測模型可以學習一種依賴於小批量的簡單啟發式，這是通過批量正則化層實現的，以解決邊緣分類任務。在進行校正後，我們觀察到網絡表示與節點分類相關特徵的對齊度增加，這表明網絡已經學習到一種與底層圖屬性更好對齊的圖表示。我們的發現表明，標準的鏈接預測訓練可能使我們高估鏈接預測器學習與任務一致的圖的通用表示的能力。
2604.25978.author: Kieran Maguire et.al.
